headline: Carr's command, Notre Dame's balance too much for UCLA in opener
body: CJ Carr looked every bit the starting quarterback Notre Dame needed him to be, threading passes with precision and managing the game's tempo in a 45-28 victory over UCLA that never felt as close as the final margin. Carr completed 24 of 36 attempts for 346 yards and four touchdowns, the kind of efficient opening statement that sets a tone for the season. He had time to operate, rarely flustered, and made one crucial decision after another in the middle of the field.

The real separation came in how Notre Dame balanced its attack. While Carr shouldered the passing load, the Irish ground game—anchored by Audric Price's 78 yards on 17 carries—kept UCLA's defense honest and prevented the kind of pass rush intensity that might have rattled a younger signal caller. Notre Dame's offensive line got movement at the point of attack, a detail that doesn't show up in the box score but showed up on every other drive. UCLA's Amon-Ra St. Brown Moore III had a strong day with five receptions for 92 yards, keeping chains moving, but it was never enough to flip momentum.

The turnover battle told its own story. Notre Dame committed zero giveaways while UCLA coughed it up once, a luxury in a game decided by 17 points. Jaylen Iamaleava threw for 282 yards and three touchdowns for the Bruins, a respectable debut in his own right, but he couldn't overcome the structural advantages Notre Dame built early. This wasn't about one player dominating; it was about Notre Dame executing a complete game while UCLA played well enough to score 28 but never well enough to keep pace.
turningPoint: Carr's two touchdown passes in the second quarter, both to different receivers, established a rhythm that left UCLA perpetually chasing rather than competing.

unsungHero.name: Audric Price
unsungHero.why: His 17 carries for 78 yards and a touchdown sustained drives and kept UCLA's pass rush from ever getting fully uncoiled, the kind of blocking-and-running grunt work that enables quarterback success but rarely garners headlines.
